operating_state: Total revenue increased 6% to $58.4 billion driven by comparable sales growth of 6% from increased shopping frequency and higher average ticket, plus 26 net new warehouses. Growth was broad-based with U.S. up modestly, Canada and Other International posting strong double-digit increases, while e-commerce accelerated 18% after prior year declines. Core merchandise categories grew across all segments while warehouse ancillary businesses increased led by pharmacy. Membership fee revenue increased 8% to $1.1 billion driven by new member sign-ups, Executive membership upgrades, and higher renewal rates.
cost_structure: Gross margin percentage improved 8 basis points to 10.80% driven by warehouse ancillary businesses contributing 6 basis points, LIFO benefit adding 3 basis points, and core merchandise contributing 2 basis points, partially offset by increased Executive member rewards reducing margin by 7 basis points. SG&A expenses as percentage of sales increased 3 basis points to 9.14% with warehouse operations higher by 8 basis points due to wage increases implemented in March and September 2023, offset by central operating cost leverage and reduced stock compensation expense.
material_events: Company paid special cash dividend of $15.00 per share totaling $6.7 billion in January 2024 and declared regular quarterly dividend of $1.02 per share compared to $0.90 prior year. Richard Galanti retired as CFO effective March 15, 2024, succeeded by Gary Millerchip from Kroger. Japan subsidiary issued $500 million in Guaranteed Senior Notes with maturities ranging from 2033 to 2043. Stock repurchases totaled $322 million in first half versus $285 million prior year.
macro_exposures: Lower global gasoline prices reduced net sales by $572 million year-to-date with average price per gallon declining 4%, generally benefiting net sales growth but negatively impacting gross margin percentage. Foreign currency translation provided modest tailwinds from Canadian and Other International operations. Company faces exposure to tariffs particularly between China and United States affecting merchandise costs with degree dependent on goods type, rates imposed, and timing.
forward_outlook: Management plans to open 16 additional new warehouses including one relocation in remainder of fiscal 2024 following 14 openings in first half, with continued square footage growth at higher rates in foreign markets. Capital expenditures expected to total $4.4 to $4.6 billion for fiscal 2024 financed through operating cash flows and existing liquidity. E-commerce business expansion continues domestically and internationally while company maintains focus on membership base growth and Executive membership penetration.
uncertainty_profile: Multiple employment-related class action lawsuits pending regarding wage and hour claims across California, New York, and New Jersey with potential material exposure. Opioid litigation consolidated in federal MDL includes claims from counties, cities, and third-party payors across multiple states. Privacy law violations alleged in class actions related to website pixel trackers while government investigations ongoing including Quebec prescription drug inquiry and U.S. False Claims Act investigation regarding prescription medications.
